title: ደግሞ አርባ ቀን እየታያቸው ስለ እግዚአብሔርም መንግሥት ነገር እየነገራቸው፥ በብዙ ማስረጃ ከሕማማቱ በኋላ ሕያው ሆኖ ለእነርሱ ራሱን አሳያቸው።
excerpt:
እነርሱም በተሰበሰቡ ጊዜ። ጌታ ሆይ፥ በዚህ ወራት ለእስራኤል መንግሥትን ትመልሳለህን? ብለው ጠየቁት።
7 እርሱም። አብ በገዛ ሥልጣኑ ያደረገውን ወራትንና ዘመናትን ታውቁ ዘንድ ለእናንተ አልተሰጣችሁም፤
8 ነገር ግን መንፈስ ቅዱስ በእናንተ ላይ በወረደ ጊዜ ኃይልን ትቀበላላችሁ፥ በኢየሩሳሌምም በይሁዳም ሁሉ በሰማርያም እስከ ምድር ዳርም ድረስ ምስክሮቼ ትሆናላችሁ አለ።
9 ይህንም ከተናገረ በኋላ እነርሱ እያዩት ከፍ ከፍ አለ፤ ደመናም ከዓይናቸው ሰውራ ተቀበለችው።
10 እርሱም ሲሄድ ወደ ሰማይ ትኵር ብለው ሲመለከቱ ሳሉ፥ እነሆ፥ ነጫጭ ልብስ የለበሱ ሁለት ሰዎች በአጠገባቸው ቆሙ፤
11 ደግሞም። የገሊላ ሰዎች ሆይ፥ ወደስማይ እየተመለከታችሁ ስለ ምን ቆማችኋል? ይህ ከእናንተ ወደ ሰማይ የወጣው ኢየሱስ ወደ ሰማይ ሲሄድ እንዳያችሁት፥ እንዲሁ ይመጣል አሉአቸው።
content:
ደግሞ አርባ ቀን እየታያቸው ስለ እግዚአብሔርም መንግሥት ነገር እየነገራቸው፥ በብዙ ማስረጃ ከሕማማቱ በኋላ ሕያው ሆኖ ለእነርሱ ራሱን አሳያቸው።
4 ከእነርሱም ጋር አብሮ ሳለ ከኢየሩሳሌም እንዳይወጡ አዘዛቸው፥ ነገር ግን። ከእኔ የሰማችሁትን አብ የሰጠውን የተስፋ ቃል ጠብቁ፤
5 ዮሐንስ በውኃ አጥምቆ ነበርና፥ እናንተ ግን ከጥቂት ቀን በኃላ በመንፈስ ቅዱስ ትጠመቃላችሁ አለ።
6 እነርሱም በተሰበሰቡ ጊዜ። ጌታ ሆይ፥ በዚህ ወራት ለእስራኤል መንግሥትን ትመልሳለህን? ብለው ጠየቁት።
7 እርሱም። አብ በገዛ ሥልጣኑ ያደረገውን ወራትንና ዘመናትን ታውቁ ዘንድ ለእናንተ አልተሰጣችሁም፤
8 ነገር ግን መንፈስ ቅዱስ በእናንተ ላይ በወረደ ጊዜ ኃይልን ትቀበላላችሁ፥ በኢየሩሳሌምም በይሁዳም ሁሉ በሰማርያም እስከ ምድር ዳርም ድረስ ምስክሮቼ ትሆናላችሁ አለ።
9 ይህንም ከተናገረ በኋላ እነርሱ እያዩት ከፍ ከፍ አለ፤ ደመናም ከዓይናቸው ሰውራ ተቀበለችው።
10 እርሱም ሲሄድ ወደ ሰማይ ትኵር ብለው ሲመለከቱ ሳሉ፥ እነሆ፥ ነጫጭ ልብስ የለበሱ ሁለት ሰዎች በአጠገባቸው ቆሙ፤
11 ደግሞም። የገሊላ ሰዎች ሆይ፥ ወደስማይ እየተመለከታችሁ ስለ ምን ቆማችኋል? ይህ ከእናንተ ወደ ሰማይ የወጣው ኢየሱስ ወደ ሰማይ ሲሄድ እንዳያችሁት፥ እንዲሁ ይመጣል አሉአቸው።
12 በዚያን ጊዜ ደብረ ዘይት ከሚባለው ተራራ ወደ ኢየሩሳሌም ተመለሱ፥ እርሱም ከኢየሩሳሌም የሰንበት መንገድ ያህል የራቀ ነው።

title: The "Coffee Budget" Myth: Why Your $7 Latte Isn’t the Enemy
excerpt:
We’ve all heard the lecture. If you just stopped buying avocado toast and expensive lattes, you’d have a down payment for a house by next Tuesday.

Here’s the truth: Micro-optimizing your life is exhausting and rarely moves the needle. If you want to actually build wealth in 2026, you need to focus on the "Big Three": Housing, Transportation, and Investing.
content:
1. Master the 50/30/20 Rule

Instead of tracking every cent, look at the big picture. This framework keeps your finances balanced without making you feel like a monk.

  • 50% Needs: Rent/Mortgage, utilities, groceries, insurance.

  • 30% Wants: Dining out, hobbies, and yes, that $7 latte.

  • 20% Savings & Debt Repayment: This is your "Future You" fund.

2. The Power of "Set and Forget"

Human willpower is a finite resource. If you have to choose to save money every month, eventually, you’ll choose a vacation or a new gadget instead.

Automation is the ultimate financial life hack. Set up a recurring transfer from your paycheck directly into a high-yield savings account or a brokerage. If you never see the money in your checking account, you won't miss it.

3. Understanding Compound Interest

Time is your greatest asset. Small amounts invested early are worth significantly more than large amounts invested late.

For example, if you want to understand the math behind why starting at 25 is better than starting at 35, the future value $FV$ of an investment is calculated as:

$$FV = P \left(1 + \frac{r}{n}\right)^{nt}$$
author: Bemnet Abebe

title: The "Lattes vs. Leverage" Debate: Why Micro-Saving is Dying
excerpt: For years, the gold standard of personal finance advice was simple: Stop buying $5 lattes, and you’ll be a millionaire. While the math of compound interest technically supports this, it ignores the psychological reality of Financial Burnout.
content:
The 80/20 Rule of Wealth

In finance, the Pareto Principle suggests that 80% of your wealth-building results come from just 20% of your decisions. Instead of agonizing over a coffee, focus on the "Big Three" wins:

  1. Housing Costs: Keeping your rent or mortgage below 25% of your take-home pay.

  2. Transportation: Avoiding the "new car smell" trap and the high-interest debt that comes with it.

  3. Automated Investing: Setting up a system where you never even see the money you're saving .

The Mathematics of "The Gap"

Your wealth isn't determined by your salary; it's determined by The Gap—the distance between what you earn and what you spend.

To maximize this, you have two levers:

  1. The Floor: Cutting expenses (this has a limit; you can't spend $0).

  2. The Ceiling: Increasing income (this is theoretically infinite).

The Insight: You can only cut so many lattes before life feels restrictive, but there is no limit to how much your skills can earn in the modern economy.

For years, the gold standard of personal finance advice was simple: Stop buying $5 lattes, and you’ll be a millionaire. While the math of compound interest technically supports this, it ignores the psychological reality of Financial Burnout.

If your WACC is 8%, but your new project only returns 6%, you are technically destroying value, even if the project looks "profitable" on a basic P&L statement. You are effectively paying 8 cents to make 6 cents.

In finance, "profit" isn't just any number above zero. To truly create value, a business must earn a return that is higher than what it cost to get the money in the first place. This is known as the Cost of Capital.
author: Sosina Kebede

title: Accounting 101: Keeping Your Small Business Audit-Ready
excerpt: For many entrepreneurs, "accounting" is a scary word that gets pushed to the bottom of the to-do list until tax season hits. However, keeping a clean set of books is less about math and more about organization.
content: The Golden Rule: Separation of Church and StateThe biggest mistake new business owners make is mixing personal and business finances. If you take away nothing else from this post, remember this: Open a dedicated business bank account. It simplifies your life by:Providing a clear "paper trail" for the IRS/tax authorities.Making it easier to track your actual profit margins.Protecting your personal assets in certain legal structures.Cash vs. Accrual: Which is for you?Choosing an accounting method determines when you record income and expenses.FeatureCash BasisAccrual BasisTimingRecords when money enters/leaves the bank.Records when the transaction occurs (invoices).ComplexityVery Simple.More Complex.AccuracyGood for seeing real-time "pocket" cash.Better for long-term financial health.Who uses it?Solopreneurs & very small shops.Growing businesses with inventory or staff.Key Financial Statements You Must UnderstandYou don't need to be a CPA, but you should be able to read these three reports:The Income Statement (P&L): Shows your revenue minus expenses over a specific period. It answers: "Did I make money this month?"The Balance Sheet: A snapshot of what you own (assets) vs. what you owe (liabilities) at a specific moment.The Cash Flow Statement: Tracks the physical movement of cash. Note: You can be profitable on paper but still run out of cash!The "Quick Math" of AccountingAt its core, all accounting is based on the Accounting Equation:$$Assets = Liabilities + Equity$$Pro-Tip: If you use cloud accounting software, set aside 15 minutes every Friday to reconcile your transactions. It prevents the "End-of-Year Panic" and keeps your data fresh.
author: Sosina Kebede
